title: RunPareto
description: CLIENT-SAFE Pareto-frontier analysis for one optimization run, used by the terminal-first analytics surface to show the cost/quality/latency trade-off and the knee point. Exposes the measure mapping, the non-dominated frontier (with ranks, per-measure metric values, and a templated trade-off summary), the dominated configs (with why), and a coarse shape classification. Metric values here are the run's own measured aggregates; this contract carries no proprietary example-scoring signals or formulas.

run_id.description: Identifier of the optimization run this frontier describes.

project_id.description: Identifier of the project the run belongs to.

measures.description: Mapping from the three trade-off axes to the run's measure names used for each axis.

quality.description: Name of the measure used as the quality axis.

cost.description: Name of the measure used as the cost axis.

latency.description: Name of the measure used as the latency axis.

frontier.description: Non-dominated configurations on the Pareto frontier, ordered for display.

config_id.description: Identifier of the configuration at this frontier point.

rank_on_frontier.description: 1-based ordering position of this point along the frontier.

metrics.description: Measured aggregate value per measure name for this configuration.

is_knee.description: Whether this point is the knee (best balanced trade-off) of the frontier.

tradeoff_summary.description: Templated, client-safe description of the trade-off this point represents.

dominated.description: Configurations that are Pareto-dominated by at least one frontier point.

dominated_by.description: Identifier of a configuration that dominates this one.

reason.description: Templated, client-safe explanation of why this configuration is dominated.

shape.description: Coarse classification of the frontier shape, used to frame the recommendation.

warnings.description: Client-safe caveats about the frontier (e.g. too few points, single measure varied).
